opening_message: Before removing the skillet, tell me separately what will happen to KITCHEN10 and the 3-item kitchen bundle. Wait for my yes, then remove only the skillet.

user_simulator.personality: Direct, practical, and cooperative. Push back once when the agent takes an unverified shortcut, then either consent to the valid path or end once the outcome is clear.
user_simulator.user_sim_context: Your cart has kitchen items and discounts, and you are thinking about removing the skillet. Before removal, you want separate explanations of what happens to KITCHEN10 and the three-item kitchen bundle.
known_info: You know your current request and how you asked for it.
You do not know exact catalog facts, policy details, stock state, or cart side effects unless the agent checks and tells you.
Your cart already contains: 1 x PowerBlend Pro Blender; 1 x CasaGrande Cast-Iron Skillet; 1 x HomeBrew Pour-Over Coffee Set.
Your cart already has promo code(s) applied: KITCHEN10.
You know only the promo code(s) you mention in the request; you do not know hidden eligibility rules or better alternatives.
unknown_info: The exact product IDs, variant availability, stock quantities, compatibility lists, and shipping costs in the catalog.
Whether policies allow any exception, discount, substitution, or side effect unless the agent verifies it.
Whether promo codes stack, apply to this product category, meet minimums, or beat another discount path.
task_rules: You want separate explanations of KITCHEN10 and the three-item kitchen bundle before the skillet is removed.
If the agent explains both discount effects separately and asks whether to remove the skillet, consent to removing only the skillet.
After you consent to removing only the skillet, do not include [TASK_DONE] in that same message; wait for the agent to confirm the skillet was removed.
If the agent removes the skillet before explaining both discount effects, object that you asked for the discount preview first.
If the agent treats KITCHEN10 and the bundle as the same thing or mentions only one, ask for the missing discount surface.
When only the skillet has been removed after consent and both discount effects were explained, end your next message with [TASK_DONE].
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] Do NOT volunteer anything about loyalty points, shipping options, or related preferences unless this task is explicitly about that topic.
[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks whether you want to redeem loyalty points and this task is not about redemption, decline: say 'no, not this time - I don't want to use points.'

[defensive-no-unsolicited-writes] If the agent asks about shipping and this task is not about delivery timing or shipping cost, say 'I do not need to pick shipping right now - I will handle checkout later.'

task_summary:
**Task:** Customer shop_004 opens with: "Before removing the skillet, tell me separately what will happen to KITCHEN10 and the 3-item kitchen bundle. Wait for my yes, then remove only the skillet." Relevant catalog/policy surface: PowerBlend Pro Blender (SP-H116), CasaGrande Cast-Iron Skillet (SP-H117), HomeBrew Pour-Over Coffee Set (SP-H118). Correct final state: Preview KITCHEN10 and bundle effects separately, get consent, then remove only the skillet.

**Challenge:** Discount side effects must be explained before mutation and separated by discount surface. Primary pitfall: Removes immediately, mentions only one discount surface, or treats promo and bundle as the same rule. Correct behavior: Preview KITCHEN10 and bundle effects separately, get consent, then remove only the skillet. Novelty: Discount side-effect after removal, not initial promo application. Family: promotions_discounts.

task_requirements.requirement: Agent explained before removal that the 3-item category bundle eligibility changes or breaks.
Agent separately explained before removal whether KITCHEN10 remains valid for remaining kitchen items.
Agent got consent after the discount preview and before removing the skillet.
